title: SetResponse HashMap keys are String not Id — needs a comment explaining why
description:
After this commit, every account_id, ids, created (Vec), destroyed (Vec) field in GetResponse/QueryResponse/ChangesResponse/SetResponse was upgraded from String to Id. However, the HashMap keys in SetResponse remain String:

  pub created: Option<HashMap<String, T>>,
  pub updated: Option<HashMap<String, T>>,
  pub not_created: Option<HashMap<String, SetError>>,
  pub not_updated: Option<HashMap<String, SetError>>,
  pub not_destroyed: Option<HashMap<String, SetError>>,

This is correct per RFC 8620 §5.3: the keys in the created/updated/not_*/destroyed maps are the caller-supplied *creation keys* (arbitrary strings chosen by the client), not server-assigned Ids. But a reader who just finished reading the fields above — all now typed Id — will wonder why these are still String.

Add a brief doc comment on the created and not_created fields explaining that the map key is the caller-supplied creation key, not a server Id. E.g.:

  /// Keys are caller-supplied creation keys (not server Ids); see RFC 8620 §5.3.
  pub created: Option<HashMap<String, T>>,

File: crates/jmap-chat/src/methods/mod.rs lines 81-86.
close_reason: Fixed: added 'Keys are caller-supplied creation keys (not server Ids); see RFC 8620 §5.3.' to created and not_created fields
